frameworks.name: multi-dimensional population coding
frameworks.one_line: decisions, context, and sensory variables coded along multiple population dimensions

frameworks.name: one-dimensional attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: single attractor axis insufficient to capture PFC decision representations

frameworks.name: rotational neural dynamics
frameworks.one_line: post-accumulation phase exhibits rotational structure during decision-making

frameworks.name: context-dependent perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: prefrontal role in selecting task-relevant sensory features under context

core_question: How does monkey prefrontal cortex population activity encode decisions across multiple dynamic dimensions?
key_assumptions: task-relevant dimensions can be identified from population activity
decision variables are encoded at the population level rather than single units
broadly tuned neurons can simultaneously support distinct coding dimensions
manipulations: context cueing which sensory feature is relevant
presentation of relevant and irrelevant sensory information
tracking representations across time within the trial
key_findings: PFC carries a multi-dimensional code for decision, context, and sensory variables
representations evolve from a linear accumulation phase to a rotational phase
coding dimensions arise from a single broadly tuned population, not distinct subpopulations
dynamic transition coincides with a plateau in decoding accuracy
interpretations: PFC decision coding is multi-dimensional and time-evolving rather than one-dimensional attractor-like
broad tuning supports multiplexed representations across decision phases
connecting_signals: duplicate of paper #547 (same DOI, truncated title)
